title: Tanker report - Week 46
summary:
Clean
The Middle East Gulf has been relatively static this week on the LRs. The TC1 index has held in the mid WS170's all week, but with Bahri week ending we are now hearing WS190 reported on subjects for this run. TC20 has begun to tick up from $3,800,000 to $3,975,000 with a $4,000,000 deal widely reported.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: The Middle East Gulf has been relatively static this week on the LRs. The TC1 index has held in the mid WS170's all week, but with Bahri week ending we are now hearing WS190 reported on subjects for this run.

children.title: VLCC
children.summary: The VLCC market continued the firmer trend this week with rates improving significantly on all the Baltic Exchange routes. 270,000 mt Middle East Gulf to China climbed 15 points (13.4%) to almost WS128 (a daily TCE round trip of $96,400).

children.title: Suezmax
children.summary: The Suezmax market in the West saw further gains in the rates, where 135,000mt CPC/Augusta is now trading 25 points up from a week ago at WS240 (a TCE showing a daily return of $113,600). Meanwhile for the 130,000mt voyage Nigeria to Rotterdam market, rates steadily rose 10 points week-on-week to WS205 (a daily TCE round-trip of $79,300).

children.title: Aframax
children.summary: The Aframax market in the US-Caribbean region saw a stratospheric improvement this week with tonnage positions tight, unrelenting cargo volume, a storm brewing and delays at terminals - as well as in the Panama canal. This enabled owners to put the squeeze on charterers.
